steps.title: Use a two-point table
steps.content: The line passes through $(0,21)$ and has slope $4$. In slope-intercept form $f(x)=mx+b$, this means $m=4$ and $b=21$.

steps.title: Write the equation using slope-intercept form
steps.content: Therefore $$f(x)=4x+21.$$ Choice D is the only option with slope $4$ and $y$-intercept $21$. The other choices either use the wrong slope or the wrong intercept, so D is correct.
